title: Quantized Hall Conductance in a Two-Dimensional Periodic Potential
canonical_scope: External source anchor for the TKNN Hall-conductance invariant and its role in the topological-phases core theorem family.

section_map.title: Main Result
section_map.summary: The Hall conductance of a gapped two-dimensional periodic system is expressed as a quantized topological invariant.

section_map.title: Kubo Formula To Topological Invariant
section_map.summary: The Hall-conductance Kubo formula is reorganized into a Brillouin-zone topological integral when the Fermi energy lies in a gap.

section_map.title: Projector / Curvature Identity
section_map.summary: The same Hall integer is written through Berry-curvature data of the occupied bundle, giving the local geometric integrand used in later projector formulas.

section_map.title: Gap-Protected Quantization
section_map.summary: Quantization is protected as long as the Fermi level remains in a spectral gap, so the integral cannot change continuously.

equation_map.label: hall-kubo-topological-integral
equation_map.title: Hall conductance rewritten as a Brillouin-zone topological integral

equation_map.label: occupied-bundle-curvature-form
equation_map.title: Occupied-bundle Berry curvature as the local TKNN density

equation_map.title: Quantized Hall integer protected by the spectral gap
